text:
FDG PET/CT — Whole Body (Skull Base to Mid-Thigh)

CLINICAL INDICATION:
New diagnosis of squamous cell carcinoma of the left base of tongue, p16-positive, in a 52-year-old never-smoking female. Referred for initial staging evaluation to assess extent of locoregional disease and evaluate for distant metastatic spread prior to initiation of definitive therapy.

TECHNIQUE:
Whole-body FDG PET/CT was performed from skull base to mid-thighs using a dedicated PET/CT scanner (3D acquisition). The patient fasted for greater than 6 hours prior to the examination. Serum glucose at the time of FDG injection was 88 mg/dL. 10.2 mCi of F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose was administered intravenously. Standard uptake period of 60 minutes elapsed prior to image acquisition. CT component performed with intravenous contrast (low-dose CT for attenuation correction and anatomic localization). Images were reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes in both PET and fused PET/CT formats.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK — PRIMARY SITE:
There is an intensely FDG-avid mass centered at the left base of tongue, measuring approximately 2.8 x 2.1 cm in axial dimension on the CT component, with an SUVmax of 14.2. The lesion demonstrates markedly elevated glucose metabolism compared to background oropharyngeal mucosa. The mass is confined to the left hemioropharynx without convincing extension across the midline. There is no apparent involvement of the epiglottis, vallecula proper, or pre-epiglottic fat space. No invasion of the extrinsic tongue musculature is appreciated. Correlates with the previously characterized primary tumor on dedicated MRI of the neck.

HEAD AND NECK — NODAL DISEASE:
There is a single intensely FDG-avid left level IIA cervical lymph node measuring 2.5 cm in short axis on CT, with an SUVmax of 11.8. The node demonstrates central photopenia on PET consistent with the central necrosis characterized on prior MRI. No perinodal soft tissue FDG activity to suggest extranodal extension. The remainder of the left-sided cervical nodal stations (levels IIB, III, IV, V) show no convincing pathologic FDG uptake. No right-sided cervical nodal involvement. No bilateral or contralateral adenopathy. No FDG-avid retropharyngeal, parapharyngeal, or parotid nodes. No supraclavicular or infraclavicular nodal uptake.

HEAD AND NECK — OTHER:
No FDG-avid lesion in the nasopharynx, hypopharynx, larynx, or oral cavity to suggest a synchronous primary. Salivary glands unremarkable. Thyroid gland without focal uptake abnormality. Skull base appears intact without erosion or abnormal tracer activity.

CHEST:
Lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules, masses, consolidation, or pleural effusion identified. No FDG-avid pulmonary lesion. Mediastinum: No pathologically enlarged or FDG-avid mediastinal lymph nodes. No hilar adenopathy. No pericardial effusion. Heart demonstrates normal physiologic myocardial uptake.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
Liver: No focal hepatic lesions. Normal hepatic parenchymal uptake. Spleen: Normal size and uniform uptake. No splenic lesions. Kidneys demonstrate normal bilateral FDG excretion. No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. No FDG-avid intra-abdominal lesion. Adrenal glands unremarkable. No ascites. Pelvis: No pathologic FDG uptake in the pelvic soft tissues, bladder wall, or pelvic lymph node stations. Uterus and adnexa: No FDG-avid lesions. Physiologic urinary tract activity noted.

SKELETAL:
No FDG-avid osseous lesion in the axial or appendicular skeleton within the field of view to suggest bone metastasis. No lytic or blastic bony changes on the CT component.

IMPRESSION:
1. Intensely FDG-avid left base of tongue mass (SUVmax 14.2), consistent with the known primary squamous cell carcinoma. The lesion is confined to the left oropharynx without evidence of contralateral involvement or deep space extension on this study.
2. Single intensely FDG-avid left level IIA cervical lymph node (SUVmax 11.8; 2.5 cm), consistent with nodal metastasis. No contralateral, bilateral, or distant nodal involvement identified.
3. No FDG-avid disease in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis. No evidence of distant metastatic spread.
4. No synchronous primary head and neck malignancy.
5. Overall, findings are consistent with locoregionally confined HPV-associated oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma involving the left base of tongue with a single ipsilateral level IIA nodal metastasis, and no detectable distant disease on this examination.
